questions.question: Explain the SCAN disk scheduling algorithm with an example. Also mention its advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: SCAN moves head in one direction servicing all requests until end
Then reverses direction and services remaining requests
Also called elevator algorithm
Advantage: Provides low variance in wait time
Disadvantage: May not provide minimum seek time, jobs at far ends wait longer

questions.question: Compare contiguous, linked, and indexed file allocation methods.
keyPoints: Contiguous: files stored in consecutive blocks - fast access but external fragmentation
Linked: each block has pointer to next - no fragmentation but no random access
Indexed: uses index block with pointers - supports random access but needs extra storage for index
Trade-offs between access time, storage efficiency, and complexity

questions.question: What is free space management? Discuss the bit vector method for free space management.
keyPoints: Free space management tracks available disk blocks
Bit vector uses an array where each bit represents a block
0 = free, 1 = allocated
Advantages: efficient to find consecutive free blocks, simple implementation
Disadvantages: requires extra memory for the bitmap

questions.question: Explain the concept of swap space management in operating systems.
keyPoints: Swap space extends RAM using secondary storage
Used when physical memory is full
OS moves inactive pages/processes to swap space
Located in dedicated disk partition or swap file
Improves multiprogramming degree but slower than RAM

questions.question: Describe the different levels of RAID and their purposes.
keyPoints: RAID 0: Striping - improves performance but no redundancy
RAID 1: Mirroring - duplicates data for fault tolerance
RAID 5: Distributed parity - balances performance and redundancy
RAID 6: Double parity - tolerates two disk failures
Each level trades off performance, capacity, and reliability

questions.question: What is buffer cache? Explain its role in improving disk I/O performance.
keyPoints: Buffer cache is memory area caching disk blocks
Recently accessed data kept in memory
Reduces physical disk access
Hits in cache avoid disk I/O entirely
Write-back and write-through strategies exist

questions.question: Differentiate between internal fragmentation and external fragmentation with examples.
keyPoints: Internal fragmentation: allocated memory is larger than requested - unused space inside allocated unit (e.g., fixed-size pages)
External fragmentation: free memory scattered in small chunks - cannot satisfy large requests (e.g., contiguous allocation after many allocations/deallocations)
Both reduce effective memory utilization

questions.question: With the help of a suitable example, explain the following disk scheduling algorithms: FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, and C-SCAN. Compare their performance in terms of throughput, seek time, and fairness.
keyPoints: FCFS: example with queue 98, 183, 37, 122, 14, 124, 65, 67 from head position 53 - calculate total head movement
SSTF: choose nearest first - may cause starvation
SCAN: elevator movement - serves requests in one direction then reverses
C-SCAN: circular - provides uniform wait time
Performance comparison: SSTF best seek time, FCFS fairest, SCAN/C-SCAN balanced
Need to show calculations for example to demonstrate differences

questions.question: Explain indexed file allocation in detail. What are its advantages and disadvantages compared to other allocation methods? How does the single-level and multi-level index allocation work?
keyPoints: Indexed allocation uses index block storing pointers to all file blocks
Each file has its own index block
Single-level: one index block with direct pointers to all blocks
Multi-level: hierarchical index blocks for larger files
Advantages: supports direct access, no external fragmentation
Disadvantage: needs extra storage for index, index block may become bottleneck
Linked and indexed combination possible

questions.question: Discuss the various free space management techniques in detail. Compare their advantages and disadvantages in terms of storage efficiency and computational complexity.
keyPoints: Bit Vector (Bitmap): simple, efficient for finding consecutive blocks but needs memory
Linked List: links free blocks together - no extra memory but inefficient for finding contiguous space
Grouping: first free block stores addresses of k other free blocks - good for finding multiple blocks
Counting: stores starting block and count of consecutive free blocks - efficient for large contiguous allocations
Comparison based on allocation speed, memory overhead, and fragmentation

questions.question: What is disk caching? Explain the difference between write-through and write-back caching strategies. Also discuss the problems associated with cache coherence in distributed systems.
keyPoints: Disk cache stores frequently accessed disk blocks in memory
Write-through: writes go to cache and disk immediately - reliable but slower
Write-back: writes go to cache first, then to disk later - faster but risk data loss if crash occurs before writing to disk
Cache coherence problem: when multiple systems have copies of same data
Solutions: write-invalidate, write-update protocols
Distributed systems face consistency challenges with cached data
